mentions.note: Memory chip plays mentioned; Intel not explicitly discussed but memory makers rallying may indicate shift in semiconductor hierarchy.
Stock flying since Lip Bhutan became CEO; reclaiming America's semiconductor mantle, but after recent run, actual earnings may not be big enough to justify valuation—give this one a rest.
Up 40% YTD despite weak quarter; demand for chips insane; CEO Pat Gelsinger has more up his sleeve per Cramer; do not give up on this stock—mistake to abandon it now.

Down 17% on Q1 sub-optimal guidance due to inability to produce CPUs fast enough despite massive shortage; execution miss not demand problem. Still great company under Lip Buu; will take advantage of CPU shortage.
Missed guidance on CPU shortage (supply constraint, not demand); down 17% but still great company under Lip Boutan. Will benefit from CPU shortage despite poor execution this quarter.
Reported good numbers but dismal guidance on CPU supply constraints; lacks manufacturing equipment; doesn't have ability to meet chip demand; signals more business for capital equipment providers.
Massive gains riding data-center wave; CPU production essential for AI platform deployment; market loves.
Mentioned as cheaper valuation than Cerebras in context of AI chip race; no new fundamental news but valuation more reasonable than hot IPO.

Lip Bu Tan's remarkable turnaround: $20→$108 in 12 months. Culture flattening, federal support ($5B Nvidia, $2B SoftBank), simplified roadmap, AI-driven CPU demand, foundry yield recovery, advanced packaging, and 14A (1.4nm) on track for 2028–2029 parity with TSMC.
Semiconductor powering AI; CEO Lip Bu Tan championed for backing semiconductor startups and valuing IP; important player but Nvidia dominates.
Did not hold up in late-day trading; weakness despite data center opportunities and Lip Bu Tan's packaging division leadership creates concern.
CPU demand explosion from agentic AI as GPU-to-CPU ratio shifts from 8:1 toward 1:1; CEO Lip Bu Tan fixing foundry business; potential Apple and Musk orders on 18A node; own it, don't trade it.

Now my favorite stock in charitable trust; memory chip shortage severe but CPU shortage coming next and that's Intel's wheelhouse. Expecting Apple deal to materialize; club aggressively recommended buying.
Currently Cramer's favorite stock; CEO Lip-Bu Tan has turned the company around with CPU leadership, chip packaging, and foundry growth opportunities.
Up 84% in 2025 on a turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, aided by roughly $9B in Trump-administration investment and $5B from Nvidia; headed in the right direction.
AI hardware beneficiary; the once-lowly chipmaker soared while software peers got crushed.
Turnaround backed by a federal stake and Nvidia's $5 billion investment remains intact; RSI is overbought but that can persist for weeks.
Up 26% in January; more than doubled from government investment stake level; CEO Lip Boutan executing; negativity overdone after last quarter.
Nvidia ecosystem investment; part of AI buildout; cyclical but ecosystem part of Nvidia's moat.
Buying back Irish facility sold to PE during crisis—sign of balance sheet strength; now rock solid after long teetering.
Hardware renaissance under Lip-Bu Tan; hardware stocks crushing software in generational shift; Intel back from the dead.
CEO Pat Gelsinger executing remarkable turnaround in CPU and high-end packaging; street graded last quarter poorly—wait to see quarter before buying.
CPU shortage emerging as AI bottleneck; up 59% since March 30 bottom; government bought at $20.47, Nvidia at $23.28 — both in huge gains; CEO Lip-Bu Tan remarkable turnaround.
CPUs needed for agentic AI; Nvidia doesn't make them; insatiable demand from AI agents replacing humans in dull/dirty jobs; Cramer regrets missing it.
Quadrupled in 9 months; new all-time high on 24% single-day gain; fourth industrial revolution (agentics) driving CPU demand; buy on pullbacks as re-rating from negative to positive continues.
CPU complex involvement; companies tied to Intel had pullback today—good entry on weakness; should perform well rest of year.
CPU shortage crippling AI agent growth; stock rallied 12% as constrained supply is exactly what market rewards in fourth industrial revolution.
1-year return +399% (vs S&P +29%). Semis rebound and competitive reset driving recovery; patience on beaten-down names rewarded.
CPU shortage ends CPU glut; both Intel and AMD now growth stocks. Data-center AI agent build-out driving insatiable demand; no near-term supply relief (equipment makers maxed out).
Chip/semi layer of AI cake; part of semiconductor infrastructure buildout.
